BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Q1 2026 check-in on Amanda's occasional freelance backend consulting side income after tightening the deductible-expense line items. Break-even model tracks whether hours + rate cover quarterly estimated taxes and home-office overhead.

CurrentState.Note: Averages across March and April 2026. May tracking higher with the Nnamdi wholesale order. Field labels follow the canonical template - MembershipDues represents recurring BigCommerce orders, DropIns are one-off Etsy sales, EquipmentSales is wholesale lot revenue, Rent is the storage unit, InstructorPay_Raj is held at 0 (no contractor), and AaronDrawFromNet is Amanda's owner draw.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Storage-unit rent climbs from $129 to $750/mo (worst-case if she upsizes to a 10x20 unit for wholesale prep)
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $1,134.94 + $621 increase = $1,755.94. At $98 avg BigCommerce AOV, $1,755.94 / $98 = 17.9 -> 18 recurring orders minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Tight. Owner draw would have to shrink unless wholesale volume covers it. Not worth upsizing without Nnamdi reorder confirmed.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Stretch case - storage rent to $850/mo with climate control add-on
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Effectively a wash. Climate control only justified if gear is sitting more than 90 days - keep current unit and rotate inventory faster instead.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is roughly 12 consulting hours/month at current rate and fixed overhead. At 24 average monthly billable hours the consulting side has solid headroom. Main risk is Ridgeline crunch weeks squeezing consulting availability - triage engagements accordingly.
ActionItems: Re-evaluate BigCommerce ROI after June - if BC orders stay above 5/mo keep it
Reach out to Nnamdi about Q3 wholesale reorder
Check Uline bulk pricing at $500+ order threshold
